task: I’m putting together a short note to a coworker about a schedule change, and I want it to be clear and easy to send. If anything in my draft seems unclear or off, please point it out and help me clean it up.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — paste your draft here, and I’ll help tighten it up.

I’ll check for:
- clarity
- tone
- anything that sounds awkward or too stiff
- whether the schedule change is stated clearly

If you want, I can also give you:
- a polished version
- a more casual version
- a more work-appropriate version

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, here’s the draft:

“Hey, just a heads up that my schedule changed for this week. I’m going to be coming in a little later on Thursday, around 10:00 instead of 8:30. I’ll still get my tasks done and I’ll make sure anything urgent is covered before then. Let me know if that causes any issues.”

If it sounds too casual or if the timing needs to be clearer, fix it up for me.

turns.agent_response:
Your draft is already clear and usable. I’d just tighten a couple spots so the timing and tone are a little cleaner.

### What I’d tweak
- **“my schedule changed for this week”** → fine, but a little vague. You already give the specific day, so you can make that the main point.
- **“coming in a little later”** → okay, but **“coming in at 10:00 instead of 8:30”** is clearer.
- **“I’ll still get my tasks done”** → good, but could be a little smoother.
- **“make sure anything urgent is covered before then”** → clear, though “handled before then” sounds a bit more natural.

### Cleaned-up version
**Hey, just a heads-up that my schedule changed this week, so I’ll be coming in at 10:00 on Thursday instead of 8:30. I’ll make sure my urgent tasks are handled before then, and I’ll still get everything done. Let me know if that causes any issues.**

### Slightly more polished / work-appropriate
**Hi [Name], just a quick heads-up that my schedule changed this week, so I’ll be coming in at 10:00 on Thursday instead of 8:30. I’ll make sure anything urgent is handled before then and will stay on top of my tasks. Please let me know if that creates any issues.**

If you want, I can also make it **more casual** or **more direct**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. The cleaner version is solid — clear, not too stiff. I’d probably use the more polished one at work. The only thing I’d maybe change is “stay on top of my tasks” to something a little more straightforward, like “I’ll make sure my work is covered.” But overall it’s fine.
